7: ---
8: 
9: # Apple Ads (formerly Apple Search Ads) Deep Analysis
10: 
11: <!-- Updated: 2026-04-13 | v1.5: Maximize Conversions, CPP-only (Creative Sets deprecated), AdAttributionKit, rebrand -->
12: <!-- Note: Apple rebranded "Apple Search Ads" to "Apple Ads" in April 2025 -->
13: 
14: ## Process
15: 
16: 1. Collect Apple Ads account data (exports from Apple Ads dashboard or pasted metrics)
17: 2. Identify active placement types (Search Results, Search Tab, Today Tab, Product Pages)
18: 3. Evaluate all applicable checks as PASS, WARNING, or FAIL
19: 4. Calculate ASA Health Score (0-100)
20: 5. Generate findings report with action plan
21: 
22: ## What to Analyze
23: 
24: ### Campaign Structure (25% weight)
25: 
26: **BOFU; Bottom of Funnel (Search Results, Exact Match brand)**
27: - Brand keyword campaign present (own app name + misspellings)
28: - Competitor campaign present (competitor app names as keywords)
29: - Category campaigns targeting high-intent generic terms (e.g. "workout app", "budget tracker")
30: 
31: **MOFU; Middle of Funnel (Search Match / broad discovery)**
32: - Search Match campaigns active in at least one ad group for discovery
33: - Search Match ad groups isolated from Exact Match (separate ad groups; never mix)
34: - Search Terms Report reviewed to mine converting queries for Exact Match promotion
35: 
36: **Campaign Architecture Rules:**
37: - Brand / Category / Competitor should be separate campaigns (different CPT bids, budgets)
38: - Search Match ad groups isolated from manual keyword ad groups; NEVER mix in same ad group
39: - Goal: let Search Match discover, then promote winners to Exact Match campaigns
40: 
41: ### Bid Health (20% weight)
42: 
43: **CPT (Cost Per Tap) vs Install Rate by Match Type:**
44: - CPT vs category benchmarks (see Benchmarks section below)
45: - TTR (Tap-Through Rate): benchmark >2.5% for Search Results, >1.5% for Search Tab
46: - Conversion Rate (tap → install): benchmark 50-65% for brand terms, 20-40% for category
47: - CPT/CPG (Cost Per Goal): compare against target CPI/CPA from MMP
48: 
49: **Bid Strategy:**
50: - Manual CPT bidding appropriate for small/new accounts
51: - **Maximize Conversions** (GA February 26, 2026): AI-powered auto-bidder using Search Match that sets optimal bids per search query in real time. Target CPA (weekly average target) replaces CPA Cap (being deprecated). Recommended daily budget: at least 5x target CPA. Two-week learning period minimum. **Current limitation**: only optimizes for installs, NOT post-install events (no trial, subscription, or ROAS optimization yet)
52: - CPA Goals available at campaign level; evaluate if conversion volume supports it (>100 installs/month per campaign)
53: - Are bids differentiated by match type? (Brand Exact > Category Exact > Search Match)
54: - Keyword-level CPT bids set, not just ad group default?
55: 
56: **Keyword Health:**
57: - Irrelevant Search Terms (from Search Match) identified and excluded via negative keywords
58: - Low-performing keywords paused or bid reduced (TTR <1% + high CPT)
59: - High-volume generic terms checked for intent quality (avoid "free apps" type queries)
60: 
61: ### Custom Product Pages (15% weight)
62: 
63: > **Creative Sets fully deprecated.** CPPs are now the sole ad variation mechanism. CPP limit doubled to **70** in October 2025.
64: 
65: **Custom Product Pages (CPP):**
66: - CPPs created in App Store Connect? (up to 70 per app as of Oct 2025)
67: - At least 3 CPP variants tested per campaign type (different value props per audience)
68: - CPP assets aligned with ad group keyword themes (e.g. fitness keywords → fitness screenshots)
69: - CPPs increase conversion rates ~8% for games, ~6.6% for non-gaming apps (per [AppTweak CPP guide](https://www.apptweak.com/en/aso-blog/guide-to-custom-product-pages-cpp))
70: - SoundCloud case study: CPPs in competitor campaigns led to 58% CR increase, 39% CPI reduction ([AppTweak case study](https://www.apptweak.com/en/case-studies/soundcloud))
71: - **Critical**: 78% of App Store search volume comes from devices with Personalized Ads off (per Apple's Q1 2022 internal data, [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2022/05/11/ios-15-users-opt-out-of-personalized-ads/)). Use creative-based targeting (CPP asset alignment) rather than demographic audience filters
72: 
73: **Default (Store Listing) Creative:**
74: - App icon, subtitle, and first 3 screenshots optimized; these show in ads by default
75: - Short description (170 chars) compelling and keyword-rich
76: - Preview video present (strongly recommended for TTR improvement)
77: 
78: **Creative Testing:**
79: - CPP performance compared: which variant has highest TTR and lowest CPI?
80: - Deep links in CPPs available on iOS/iPadOS 18+ (test for re-engagement)
81: - CPPs can now be assigned organic keywords (WWDC 2025), bridging paid/organic optimization
82: 
83: ### Attribution & MMP Health (15% weight)
84: 
85: **MMP Integration (Critical):**
86: - MMP (AppsFlyer / Adjust / Branch / Singular) integrated with Apple Ads via AdAttributionKit + ATT
87: - Apple Ads properly connected as a partner in MMP dashboard
88: - In-app events being sent back to Apple Ads (enables Maximize Conversions and ROAS optimization)
89: - Post-install event quality: are purchase, subscription_start, or other revenue events tracked?
90: 
91: **AdAttributionKit & Dual Attribution (April 10, 2025):**
92: - Apple Ads registered with AdAttributionKit (SKAN v1-3), creating dual attribution for the first time
93: - Installs now report through BOTH SKAN/AAK postbacks AND the AdServices API
94: - WWDC 2025: configurable attribution windows, overlapping re-engagement windows, attribution cooldowns, and country codes in postbacks ([Apple Developer, WWDC25 session 221](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/221/); [Singular recap](https://www.singular.net/blog/wwdc-2025-aak/))
95: - SKAdNetwork conversion values configured in MMP (maps user actions to conversion windows)
96: - ATT opt-in rate monitored (low ATT rate = less MMP data, more reliance on SKAN/AAK)
97: - Privacy threshold considerations: are campaigns getting postbacks or null reports?
98: 
99: **Attribution Windows:**
100: - Default Apple Ads attribution: 30-day click, 1-day view; appropriate for app install goals?
101: - WWDC 2025 added configurable windows and overlapping re-engagement windows (iOS 18.4+, requires `EligibleForAdAttributionKitOverlappingConversions=YES` in Info.plist; see [WWDC25 session 221](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/221/))
102: - For re-engagement or subscription goals: evaluate longer lookback windows
103: 
104: ### Budget Pacing (10% weight)
105: 
106: - Daily cap set at campaign level (budget pacing in ASA is daily, not monthly)
107: - Actual daily spend vs daily cap ratio: flag if consistently hitting cap (could be missing volume)
108: - Conversely: flag if spend is <50% of daily cap (creative or bid issue, not budget)
109: - Budget split across placement types aligned with performance (don't over-invest in underperforming placements)
110: - Lifetime budget campaigns (if used): check end dates and pacing curves
111: 
112: ### TAP Coverage: Placement Types (10% weight)
113: 
114: ASA offers 4 placement types; evaluate coverage and performance:
115: 
116: | Placement | Where | Best for | Benchmark CPT |
117: |-----------|-------|----------|----------------|
118: | Search Results | Below search results | High intent, bottom funnel | $0.50-$3.00 |
119: | Search Tab | Top of Search tab | Discovery, mid funnel | $0.30-$1.50 |
120: | Today Tab | App Store home | Brand awareness | $1.00-$5.00 |
121: | Product Pages | Competitor/related app pages | Competitor conquesting | $0.50-$2.00 |
122: 
123: **Evaluation:**
124: - Search Results: must be active (highest intent placement)
125: - Search Tab: active for scale? Evaluate CPT and TTR vs Search Results
126: - Today Tab: only if budget >$3k/month and brand awareness is a goal (high CPT, low intent)
127: - Product Pages: competitive opportunity; are competitor CPPs being targeted?
128: 
129: ### Goal CPA / KPI Assessment (5% weight)
130: 
131: **Benchmarks by Category (2025-2026 ASA averages):**
132: | Category | Avg CPT | Avg TTR | Avg Install CVR | Target CPI |
133: |----------|---------|---------|-----------------|------------|
134: | Games | $0.50-$1.00 | 3-5% | 55-70% | $1.00-$3.00 |
135: | Health & Fitness | $1.50-$3.00 | 2-4% | 45-60% | $3.00-$8.00 |
136: | Productivity | $1.00-$2.50 | 2-3.5% | 50-65% | $2.00-$5.00 |
137: | Finance | $2.00-$5.00 | 1.5-3% | 40-55% | $5.00-$15.00 |
138: | Education | $1.00-$2.00 | 2-4% | 50-65% | $2.00-$6.00 |
139: | Shopping | $0.80-$2.00 | 2.5-4% | 45-60% | $2.00-$5.00 |
140: | Lifestyle | $0.80-$1.80 | 2-3.5% | 45-60% | $2.00-$5.00 |
141: 
142: **Country-level benchmarks:**
143: - Tier 1 (US, UK, AU, CA, JP): CPT 2-3× above global average; highest LTV
144: - Tier 2 (DE, FR, KR, SG, HK): CPT 1-1.5× above global average
145: - Tier 3 (BR, IN, MX): CPT 30-60% below Tier 1; high volume, lower LTV
146: 
147: **Checks:**
148: - Actual CPI vs target CPI (from MMP); flag if >2x target
149: - CPI trend over 30 days (improving or worsening?)
150: - Revenue events: is ROAS positive within MMP attribution window?
151: 
152: ### Overall Benchmarks (2025 SplitMetrics data)
153: 
154: | Metric | Search Results Average |
155: |--------|-----------------------|
156: | TTR (Tap-Through Rate) | 9.7% |
157: | Conversion Rate | 66.2% |
158: | CPT (Cost Per Tap) | $2.25 |
159: | CPA (Cost Per Acquisition) | $3.76 |
160: 
161: - US is the highest-cost market
162: - AMEI (Africa/Middle East/India) is most cost-efficient and stable
163: - **International markets often deliver 3-5x better CPI than US** with comparable LTV for subscription apps
164: 
165: ### Platform Changes (v1.5)
166: 
167: | ID | Check | Severity | Notes |
168: |----|-------|----------|-------|
169: | ASA-MA1 | Multiple ads per query readiness | Medium | Rolling out March 2026: up to 2 ads per search query (was 1). Changes competitive dynamics: more search results real estate available. Evaluate bid strategy for increased competition |
170: 
171: **Deprecated:**
172: - Creative Sets: fully deprecated. Only CPPs now (up to 70 per app)
173: - CPA Cap: being retired in favor of Target CPA via Maximize Conversions
174: - Demographic audience targeting as primary strategy: 78% of App Store search volume comes from devices with Personalized Ads off (Apple's Q1 2022 internal data; conversion rate is nearly identical between opted-in and opted-out users — 62.1% vs 62.5%)
175: 
176: ## Output Format
177: 
178: ```
179: ## Apple Ads Audit
180: 
181: **ASA Health Score: [X]/100**
182: 
183: ### Critical Issues ([count])
184: - [Issue with specific impact and fix]
185: 
186: ### High Priority ([count])
187: - [Issue]
188: 
189: ### Campaign Structure
190: PASS/WARNING/FAIL for each check category
191: 
192: ### Benchmark Comparison
193: [Metric] | Your Account | ASA Benchmark | Status
194: 
195: ### Quick Wins (do this week)
196: 1. [Most impactful fix with expected outcome]
197: 2.
198: 3.
199: 
200: ### Recommended Next Steps
201: [Prioritized action plan]
202: ```
203: 
204: ## Scoring Weights
205: 
206: | Category | Weight |
207: |----------|--------|
208: | Campaign Structure | 25% |
209: | Bid Health | 20% |
210: | Custom Product Pages | 15% |
211: | Attribution & MMP | 15% |
212: | Budget Pacing | 10% |
213: | TAP Coverage | 10% |
214: | Goal KPI Assessment | 5% |
215: 
216: ## Data to Request from User
217: 
218: If not provided, ask for:
219: - Campaign list with spend, installs, CPT, TTR, CVR (last 30 days)
220: - Active placement types
221: - MMP being used (AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Singular, or none)
222: - Target CPI / CPA and app category
223: - Countries/regions active
224: - Whether Custom Product Pages are set up in App Store Connect

(End of file - total 224 lines)
</content>

# Apple Ads (formerly Apple Search Ads) Deep Analysis

<!-- Updated: 2026-04-13 | v1.5: Maximize Conversions, CPP-only (Creative Sets deprecated), AdAttributionKit, rebrand -->
<!-- Note: Apple rebranded "Apple Search Ads" to "Apple Ads" in April 2025 -->

## Process

1. Collect Apple Ads account data (exports from Apple Ads dashboard or pasted metrics)
2. Identify active placement types (Search Results, Search Tab, Today Tab, Product Pages)
3. Evaluate all applicable checks as PASS, WARNING, or FAIL

<content>
1: ---
2: name: ads-amazon
3: description: "Amazon Ads deep analysis covering Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands (incl. Sponsored Brands Video), Sponsored Display (audiences + contextual), and basic Amazon DSP. Evaluates campaign structure, ACOS/TACOS targets, search-term harvesting, negative keyword discipline, Brand Analytics signals, day-parting, bid management, auto vs manual campaign mix, ASIN targeting, and DSP retargeting. Use when user says Amazon Ads, Amazon advertising, Amazon PPC, Amazon search ads, Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display, Amazon DSP, ACOS, TACOS, retail media audit, Amazon Marketing Services, AMS, or Amazon seller advertising."
4: user-invokable: false
5: tested_date: 2026-05-17
6: tested_with: claude-code v2.x
7: ---
8: 
9: # Amazon Ads Deep Analysis
10: 
11: Amazon owns ~80% of US retail media (~$56B in 2025). This sub-skill audits
12: the three Sponsored ad types plus basic DSP visibility for sellers and
13: vendors. Detailed Amazon DSP audit (programmatic / Twitch / Fire TV / Freevee
14: / Prime Video) is queued for Wave 3 in `ads-retail-media`.
15: 
16: > **Note:** This is a leaf sub-skill. The thresholds and rules below live
17: > inline. In Wave 3, once `ads-walmart` ships and the `ads-retail-media`
18: > orchestrator extracts the shared retail-media logic, a dedicated
19: > `retail-media-specs.md` reference file will replace these inline rules
20: > (planned, not yet created — do not link to it from other files).
21: 
22: ## Process
23: 
24: 1. Collect Amazon Advertising export: Search Term Report (last 60 days),
25:    Campaign Performance Report, Targeting Report, Placement Report, Bulk
26:    Operations file
27: 2. Collect Brand Analytics if available (Brand-Registered sellers / vendors):
28:    Search Terms, Repeat Purchase Behavior, Item Comparison
29: 3. Verify Seller / Vendor Central context (vendor strategy differs from
30:    seller strategy on portfolio, coupons, and Sponsored Brands video)
31: 4. Read `ads/references/benchmarks.md` for Amazon-specific benchmarks
32: 5. Read `ads/references/scoring-system.md` for weighted scoring
33: 6. Evaluate checks per category
34: 7. Calculate Amazon Ads Health Score (0-100)
35: 8. Generate findings report with action plan
36: 
37: ## What to Analyze
38: 
39: ### Campaign Structure & Portfolios (15% weight)
40: 
41: - **Portfolio organization** — campaigns grouped by funnel stage (Awareness /
42:   Consideration / Conversion) or by product line, not ad-hoc
43: - **Campaign naming convention** consistent (e.g.,
44:   `SP_Brand_Exact_USD_Conv` / `SB_NonBrand_HSA_Video_USD_Cons`)
45: - **Auto vs Manual mix**: Sponsored Products has ≥1 Auto campaign per ASIN
46:   cluster for search-term harvesting (auto-mining workflow)
47: - **ASIN-level coverage**: every priority ASIN appears in ≥1 SP campaign
48:   (Exact, Phrase, or Broad) AND ≥1 Auto campaign
49: - **Targeting type mix in SP**: Manual Keyword + Manual Product (ASIN
50:   targeting) + Auto (for harvesting). Pure Auto is a learning-only state
51: - **Sponsored Brands**: at least one HSA (Headline Search Ad) campaign per
52:   brand storefront; SB Video tested for high-AOV products
53: - **Sponsored Display**: separate Audience (retargeting + lookalike) and
54:   Contextual (product / category targeting) campaigns — don't mix in one
55: 
56: ### Search-Term Harvesting & Negatives (25% weight)
57: 
58: - **Auto → Manual harvest cadence** weekly or bi-weekly: pull converting
59:   search terms from Auto, promote winners to Manual Exact in their target
60:   campaign, add as negatives to the Auto so spend redirects
61: - **Negative keyword coverage**: every Auto campaign has Manual campaign
62:   ASINs as negatives (prevents Auto cannibalizing Manual placements)
63: - **Negative product targeting**: irrelevant ASINs added at campaign level
64:   (prevents Sponsored Display contextual targeting from showing your ad
65:   next to incompatible products)
66: - **Search Term Report harvested last 7 days** — any high-spend, zero-
67:   conversion terms above $10 / 30 days should be negatives
68: - **Brand defense**: branded search terms are isolated in a separate
69:   campaign with exact-match brand keywords and competitor-name negatives
70: - **Top-of-search visibility** — branded search auction CPC vs default; flag
71:   if competitor bid is above your brand-defense bid
72: 
73: ### ACOS / TACOS Discipline (20% weight)
74: 
75: - **ACOS targets set per portfolio** based on contribution margin (target
76:   ACOS = (1 - contribution_margin) × buffer). Sloppy "30% across the board"
77:   ACOS targets cap performance
78: - **TACOS (Total ACOS) trending down** quarter-over-quarter — TACOS measures
79:   total ad spend / total revenue including organic; downward trend means
80:   paid is lifting organic, not replacing it
81: - **ROAS reported alongside ACOS** for the team that thinks in ROAS
82: - **Coupon + Lightning Deal stacking** — when on, ACOS targets relaxed to
83:   capture lift; auto-flag campaigns that need temporary ACOS uplift
84: - **Day-parting**: bid adjustments by hour for ASINs where add-to-cart
85:   hours differ from purchase hours (frequent for high-AOV electronics)
86: 
87: ### Bid & Budget Management (15% weight)
88: 
89: - **Dynamic bidding strategy**: `Up and Down` for Manual Exact converting
90:   campaigns; `Down Only` for Auto campaigns or Sponsored Display contextual
91:   (avoid bid escalation in learning campaigns)
92: - **Placement bid multipliers**: Top-of-search placement adjusted based on
93:   ROAS by placement (Placement Report); often +50-100% for top-of-search,
94:   -30% for product page placements
95: - **Budget caps appropriate** — no campaigns capped at <2x current daily
96:   spend (caps strangle algorithm learning)
97: - **Bulk bid adjustments** — bid optimization runs weekly via Bulk
98:   Operations not ad-hoc in the UI
99: 
100: ### Sponsored Brands (10% weight)
101: 
102: - **HSA (Headline Search Ad)**: brand logo + 3-product showcase + landing
103:   page (Store, custom URL, or product list)
104: - **SB Video**: 6-45s; 15-30s sweet spot. Hook within 2s. Captions on.
105:   Required for high-AOV products
106: - **Brand Store linked** as landing destination where available (higher
107:   CVR than product detail page)
108: - **Negative keywords on SB** include competitor brand names if you don't
109:   want top-of-funnel competitor traffic; remove if you do
110: 
111: ### Sponsored Display (10% weight)
112: 
113: - **Audience targeting**: Views Remarketing, Purchase Remarketing, Cross-
114:   Sell, and Lookalike audiences each scaled with appropriate bids
115: - **Contextual targeting**: relevant categories AND specific ASIN targeting
116:   (your own complementary products + competitor ASINs you can win)
117: - **Display creative quality**: at minimum, the default product creative;
118:   custom creative tested for top SKUs
119: - **Off-Amazon Sponsored Display** (Twitch, IMDb, third-party sites) audited
120:   separately — different CPC dynamics, different attribution windows
121: 
122: ### Brand Analytics & Reporting (5% weight)
123: 
124: - **Brand Analytics** accessed (Brand-Registered only): Top Search Terms,
125:   Item Comparison, Repeat Purchase Behavior
126: - **Top Search Terms report** used to identify share-of-voice gaps vs
127:   competitors on category keywords
128: - **Repeat Purchase Behavior** informs subscription / replenishment ad
129:   strategy
130: - **Amazon Attribution** (off-Amazon traffic) tracked if running external
131:   ads driving to Amazon listings
132: 
133: ## Key Thresholds
134: 
135: | Metric | Pass | Warning | Fail |
136: |--------|------|---------|------|
137: | ACOS (vs target) | Within ±10% of target | 10-25% over | >25% over |
138: | TACOS trend (90d) | Decreasing | Flat | Increasing |
139: | Auto / Manual mix | Both active per ASIN | Only Manual or only Auto | Single campaign / ASIN |
140: | Negative keywords on Auto | Manual ASIN keywords added | Some missing | None |
141: | Search term harvest cadence | Weekly | Monthly | Ad-hoc |
142: | Branded campaign ACOS | <5% | 5-15% | >15% |
143: | Dynamic bidding strategy | Up-and-Down on converters, Down-Only on learners | Mixed inconsistently | All campaigns same strategy |
144: | Sponsored Brands video tested | Active on top 10 ASINs | Active on 1-3 ASINs | Not tested |
145: | Brand Analytics accessed | Weekly | Monthly | Never |
146: 
147: ## Output
148: 
149: ### Amazon Ads Health Score
150: 
151: ```
152: Amazon Ads Health Score: XX/100 (Grade: X)
153: 
154: Campaign Structure:     XX/100  ████████░░  (15%)
155: Search-Term Harvesting: XX/100  ██████████  (25%)
156: ACOS / TACOS Discipline:XX/100  █████████░  (20%)
157: Bid & Budget Mgmt:      XX/100  ████████░░  (15%)
158: Sponsored Brands:       XX/100  ███████░░░  (10%)
159: Sponsored Display:      XX/100  ███████░░░  (10%)
160: Brand Analytics:        XX/100  █████░░░░░  (5%)
161: ```
162: 
163: ### Deliverables
164: 
165: - `AMAZON-ADS-REPORT.md`: Full per-category findings
166: - Search-term harvest plan: keywords to promote to Manual + keywords to
167:   add as negatives, ranked by spend recovery $
168: - ACOS-by-ASIN matrix highlighting unprofitable SKUs
169: - Sponsored Brands video opportunity list (top 10 ASINs without SB Video)
170: - Pre-launch checklist when entering a new product category
171: - Amazon DSP entry recommendation (yes / no based on total monthly spend
172:   and audience size)

(End of file - total 172 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: ---
2: name: ads-attribution
3: description: "Cross-platform attribution health audit covering AdAttributionKit (iOS view-through 24h post-impression, WWDC 2025 configurable windows), GA4 attribution models (data-driven vs last-click), Consent Mode V2 enforcement, server-side attribution stitching, MMP integration health, and cross-device / cross-platform attribution. Use when user says attribution audit, attribution model, AdAttributionKit, AAK, view-through attribution, GA4 attribution, Consent Mode V2, conversion window, attribution window, MMP audit, AppsFlyer audit, Adjust audit, Branch audit, Singular audit, cross-device attribution, or cross-platform attribution."
4: user-invokable: false
5: tested_date: 2026-05-17
6: tested_with: claude-code v2.x
7: ---
8: 
9: # Cross-Platform Attribution Health Audit
10: 
11: Attribution decay is the silent revenue killer of 2026 — Consent Mode V2 EEA
12: enforcement (Jul 21, 2025), iOS ATT, SKAdNetwork → AdAttributionKit migration,
13: and the death of third-party cookies have moved every advertiser's
14: attribution signal toward modeled, server-side, first-party data. Misaligned
15: attribution windows or unverified Consent Mode setups will mis-attribute
16: 15-40% of conversions and silently waste budget.
17: 
18: ## Process
19: 
20: 1. Collect current attribution stack: GA4 property ID, Google Ads conversion
21:    actions, Meta CAPI config, Apple Ads / AdAttributionKit registration,
22:    MMP dashboard (AppsFlyer / Adjust / Branch / Singular), any sGTM container
23: 2. Read `ads/references/conversion-tracking.md` for the cross-platform
24:    tracking baseline
25: 3. Evaluate attribution health per surface (web, iOS app, Android app,
26:    server-side)
27: 4. Score each surface PASS / WARNING / FAIL
28: 5. Generate findings report with cross-channel attribution map and remediation
29:    plan
30: 
31: ## What to Analyze
32: 
33: ### iOS Attribution (AdAttributionKit + ATT)
34: 
35: - **AdAttributionKit registered** with Apple Ads (post-Apr 10, 2025 cutover);
36:   registration creates dual attribution with SKAdNetwork (SKAN v1-3)
37: - **View-through attribution** active — 24h post-impression view window
38:   configured on Apple Ads campaigns where applicable
39: - **Configurable attribution windows** (WWDC 2025): per-campaign window
40:   customization audited; overlapping re-engagement windows used for
41:   subscription / re-acquisition campaigns
42: - **Country code in postbacks** (WWDC 2025): enabled if you need geo
43:   attribution detail
44: - **ATT (App Tracking Transparency) opt-in rate** monitored; <30% opt-in
45:   means heavy reliance on SKAN/AAK + privacy threshold
46: - **Privacy threshold awareness** — low-volume campaigns may receive null
47:   postbacks; campaign consolidation recommended below 1k installs/week
48: 
49: ### Web Attribution (GA4 + Google Ads + Meta CAPI)
50: 
51: - **GA4 attribution model**: Data-Driven (default for properties with enough
52:   data) vs Last-Click — confirm setting is intentional, not residue from a
53:   pre-2026 migration
54: - **Google Ads attribution model**: Data-Driven default; per-conversion
55:   override allowed but audit any Last-Click overrides for justification
56: - **Cross-channel attribution** in GA4: confirm Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn,
57:   TikTok, Microsoft are integrated as platforms with consent + auto-tagging
58: - **Conversion windows per channel** appropriate to sales cycle:
59:   - E-commerce: 7-day click, 1-day view
60:   - B2B / lead gen: 30-90 day click, no view
61:   - SaaS subscription: 30 day click, 1-day view, plus offline conversion
62:     import for the activation event
63: - **Conversion lag analysis** run quarterly to validate window choice
64: 
65: ### Consent Mode V2 (EU/EEA + recommended globally)
66: 
67: - **Consent Mode V2 active** (enforcement began Jul 21, 2025 for EEA/UK)
68: - **Advanced Mode** (not Basic) — Basic loses ~25% of EEA conversion signal
69: - **700+ ad clicks/day over 7 days per country/domain** threshold met for
70:   Advanced Mode behavioral modeling to activate
71: - **Signal recovery measured**: aim 15-25% conversion recovery vs pre-CMV2
72:   baseline
73: - **Consent banner** correctly reads CMV2 signals (not just GDPR boilerplate)
74: 
75: ### Server-Side Attribution Stitching
76: 
77: - **First-party server logs** stored with `user_id` + `event_id` + timestamp
78:   + platform tag for every conversion event
79: - **MMP + first-party stitching**: MMP (AppsFlyer / Adjust / Branch /
80:   Singular) receives both client-side AND server-side events with shared
81:   `event_id` for deduplication
82: - **Server-side conversion import** to Google Ads (offline conversion import)
83:   and Meta (CAPI) within 72h of the conversion event
84: - **Hash quality**: email / phone fields SHA-256 hashed and lowercased
85:   before send; cross-platform hashing convention consistent
86: - **Deduplication rate** ≥90% (matched event_id between client + server)
87: 
88: ### MMP Health (Mobile Apps)
89: 
90: - **MMP integrated** — AppsFlyer / Adjust / Branch / Singular set up before
91:   any paid campaigns launched
92: - **Apple Ads connected as a partner** in MMP dashboard
93: - **Post-install events sent back** to Apple Ads, Google UAC, Meta App
94:   Campaigns, TikTok (enables Maximize Conversions and ROAS bidding)
95: - **Event quality**: purchase, subscription_start, trial_start, or other
96:   revenue events tracked (not just install → registration)
97: - **Postback configuration**: SKAN/AAK conversion values map to meaningful
98:   user actions
99: 
100: ### Cross-Device & Cross-Platform Attribution
101: 
102: - **Customer Match** lists synced to Google Ads, Meta (Customer File CA),
103:   LinkedIn Matched Audiences, TikTok Customer File for cross-device stitching
104: - **Enhanced Conversions** active for Google Ads (hashed first-party data,
105:   ~10% uplift on properly-implemented setups)
106: - **CAPI customer_information parameters** (`em`, `ph`, `fn`, `ln`, `ct`,
107:   `st`, `zp`, `external_id`) sent server-side to Meta
108: - **Privacy-safe identifiers** (Customer Match hashes, GAID/IDFA where
109:   permitted) included in conversion exports
110: 
111: ## Key Thresholds
112: 
113: | Metric | Pass | Warning | Fail |
114: |--------|------|---------|------|
115: | GA4 attribution model | Data-Driven | Last-Click (intentional) | Last-Click (residual) |
116: | Consent Mode V2 | Advanced + verified | Advanced (unverified) | Basic / Not implemented |
117: | EMQ (Meta Purchase) | ≥8.0 | 6.0-7.9 | <6.0 |
118: | Event dedup rate | ≥90% | 70-89% | <70% |
119: | Server-side / client-side hit ratio | ≥80% | 50-79% | <50% |
120: | ATT opt-in (iOS) | ≥30% | 15-29% | <15% (heavy SKAN reliance) |
121: | Offline conversion import latency | <24h | 24-72h | >72h |
122: | Customer Match list freshness | <7 days | 7-30 days | >30 days |
123: 
124: ## Output
125: 
126: ### Attribution Health Score
127: 
128: ```
129: Attribution Health Score: XX/100 (Grade: X)
130: 
131: iOS (AdAttributionKit + ATT):    XX/100  ████████░░  (20%)
132: Web (GA4 + Ads + CAPI):           XX/100  ██████████  (30%)
133: Consent Mode V2:                  XX/100  █████████░  (15%)
134: Server-Side Stitching:            XX/100  ████████░░  (20%)
135: MMP Health (mobile):              XX/100  ███████░░░  (10%)
136: Cross-Device / Customer Match:    XX/100  ██████░░░░  (5%)
137: ```
138: 
139: ### Deliverables
140: 
141: - `ATTRIBUTION-AUDIT.md`: Full surface-by-surface findings
142: - Cross-channel attribution map (which platform owns which conversion
143:   windows + which events)
144: - Modeled vs reported conversion delta (estimated revenue under- or
145:   over-attribution)
146: - Quick Wins sorted by signal-recovery $ impact
147: - Pre-launch checklist for any new platform (Amazon, Apple Ads, TikTok)
148:   to ensure attribution is wired before spend begins

(End of file - total 148 lines)
</content>

# Cross-Platform Attribution Health Audit

Attribution decay is the silent revenue killer of 2026 — Consent Mode V2 EEA
enforcement (Jul 21, 2025), iOS ATT, SKAdNetwork → AdAttributionKit migration,
and the death of third-party cookies have moved every advertiser's
attribution signal toward modeled, server-side, first-party data. Misaligned
attribution windows or unverified Consent Mode setups will mis-attribute
15-40% of conversions and silently waste budget.

## Process

1. Collect current attribution stack: GA4 property ID, Google Ads conversion

1: ---
2: name: ads-server-side-tracking
3: description: "Server-side tracking pipeline audit covering server-side Google Tag Manager (sGTM), Meta CAPI Gateway, Conversions API health, event deduplication via event_id, server-side hit ratio targets, pixel debugging, and PII hashing discipline. Use when user says server-side tracking, sGTM, server-side GTM, server-side tagging, CAPI, Conversions API, CAPI Gateway, Meta Conversions API, event deduplication, event_id, pixel debug, pixel health, Pixel/CAPI audit, first-party tracking, iOS 14.5 recovery, or server-side hit ratio."
4: user-invokable: false
5: tested_date: 2026-05-17
6: tested_with: claude-code v2.x
7: ---
8: 
9: # Server-Side Tracking Pipeline Audit
10: 
11: Audits the entire server-side measurement pipeline that backs every paid
12: channel's modeled conversion data. Without server-side tracking in 2026,
13: expect 30-40% conversion data loss from iOS ATT, ITP, and aggressive ad
14: blockers — that's the gap between what's actually happening and what your
15: bid algorithms can see.
16: 
17: This sub-skill is technical and deep. It's NOT the same as `ads-attribution`,
18: which audits the *attribution model* sitting on top of these events.
19: 
20: ## Process
21: 
22: 1. Collect server-side stack inventory: sGTM container info, Meta CAPI
23:    integration method (Gateway / direct / partner integration), event
24:    schema documentation, hosting infrastructure (Cloud Run / GCS / AWS)
25: 2. Read `ads/references/conversion-tracking.md` for cross-platform baseline
26: 3. Test event flow: trigger known events → verify they appear in BOTH
27:    client-side (Pixel Helper / Tag Assistant) AND server-side (Events
28:    Manager test events / GA4 DebugView)
29: 4. Audit deduplication, hashing, and parameter completeness
30: 5. Score health PASS / WARNING / FAIL per surface
31: 6. Generate findings report
32: 
33: ## What to Analyze
34: 
35: ### Server-Side Google Tag Manager (sGTM)
36: 
37: - **sGTM container deployed** — hosted on Cloud Run, GCS, App Engine, or
38:   custom infrastructure. Self-hosted preferred over Google-managed for cost
39:   and data residency
40: - **Custom domain configured** (`tags.example.com`) — first-party domain
41:   avoids ITP / ad-blocker blocking that hits googletagmanager.com
42: - **Client-side GTM forwards to sGTM** correctly; cookies, IP, user-agent
43:   preserved
44: - **GA4 events flow via sGTM** (no direct client → GA4 fallback)
45: - **Conversion Linker tag** enabled — preserves Google click IDs (gclid,
46:   gbraid, wbraid) across cross-domain navigation
47: - **Server-side privacy filters** — strip non-essential PII before forwarding
48:   to analytics; only hash + forward what's needed for matching
49: 
50: ### Meta CAPI / CAPI Gateway
51: 
52: - **CAPI active** — Conversions API server-to-server alongside the Pixel
53: - **CAPI Gateway** preferred over manual server implementation (auto-
54:   hashing, parameter coverage, lower maintenance)
55: - **All major events server-side**: PageView, ViewContent, AddToCart,
56:   InitiateCheckout, Purchase, Lead, CompleteRegistration
57: - **Event Match Quality (EMQ) ≥8.0** for Purchase — confirm via Events
58:   Manager → Overview → Data sources
59: - **customer_information parameters** sent server-side: `em` (email), `ph`
60:   (phone), `fn`/`ln` (name), `ct`/`st`/`zp` (geo), `external_id`,
61:   `client_ip_address`, `client_user_agent`, `fbc`, `fbp`
62: - **Hashing**: lowercased + trimmed SHA-256 for PII fields BEFORE send
63: - **action_source** field set per event (`website`, `app`, `physical_store`,
64:   `email`, `system_generated`)
65: 
66: ### Event Deduplication
67: 
68: - **event_id** generated client-side, included in BOTH the Pixel event AND
69:   the CAPI / sGTM payload — Meta + Google both dedupe on this
70: - **Dedup rate ≥90%** measured in Events Manager → Diagnostics
71: - **Timestamp alignment** — server-side event timestamp within 5 minutes
72:   of client-side counterpart
73: - **event_name consistency** — server-side uses the same canonical event
74:   names as client-side (don't rename in transit)
75: 
76: ### Server-Side Hit Ratio
77: 
78: - **Server-side ≥80% of client-side hits** for Purchase / Lead — anything
79:   lower means iOS / ITP / ad-blocker data loss isn't being recovered
80: - **Server-side >100% acceptable** — means server-side captures conversions
81:   the client-side missed (good — that's what server-side is for)
82: - **Hit ratio monitored over time** — drops below 60% indicate broken
83:   server-side firing or missing event_id
84: 
85: ### Pixel / Tag Debug Walkthrough
86: 
87: When deployed, validate every event end-to-end:
88: 
89: - **Facebook Pixel Helper** (Chrome extension) shows the Pixel firing
90:   client-side with correct event_name + event_id + value + currency
91: - **Meta Events Manager → Test Events** shows the CAPI event arriving server-
92:   side with matching event_id and customer_information parameters populated
93: - **Google Tag Assistant** confirms client-side gtag firing
94: - **GA4 DebugView** confirms server-side event arriving with event params
95: - **Network tab** shows client → sGTM forwarding (not client → Google direct)
96: - **`window.dataLayer`** populates expected variables before any tag fires
97: 
98: ### Custom Event Taxonomy
99: 
100: - **Canonical event names** documented (e.g., `purchase` not `Purchase` or
101:   `PURCHASE` or `order_complete`)
102: - **Standard params per event**: `value`, `currency`, `content_ids`,
103:   `content_type`, `num_items`
104: - **Custom params namespaced** (`cx_segment`, `cx_funnel_step`) to avoid
105:   collision with platform-standard params
106: - **Schema versioned** — when the taxonomy changes, bump a version param
107:   so downstream platforms can handle the cutover
108: 
109: ### Hash Quality & PII Handling
110: 
111: - **Email**: lowercased + trimmed + SHA-256 (no other normalization)
112: - **Phone**: E.164 format + SHA-256 (e.g., `+15551234567`)
113: - **Name**: lowercased + trimmed + SHA-256 per first / last separately
114: - **City / state / zip**: lowercased + SHA-256
115: - **NEVER hash already-hashed values** — double-hashing breaks matching
116: - **NEVER send plain PII server-side** — only hashed
117: - **GDPR / CPRA / CDPA compliance**: confirm consent state is read before
118:   sending PII server-side, even hashed
119: 
120: ## Key Thresholds
121: 
122: | Metric | Pass | Warning | Fail |
123: |--------|------|---------|------|
124: | sGTM custom domain | Active | Configured, not active | Not configured |
125: | CAPI Gateway | Active | Manual CAPI | Pixel-only |
126: | EMQ (Purchase) | ≥8.0 | 6.0-7.9 | <6.0 |
127: | Dedup rate | ≥90% | 70-89% | <70% |
128: | Server / client hit ratio | 80-120% | 50-79% | <50% |
129: | customer_information completeness | 6+ params | 4-5 params | <4 params |
130: | Hash convention | Documented + verified | Implicit | Inconsistent |
131: | Test events validation | All 6 events pass | 3-5 events pass | <3 events pass |
132: 
133: ## Output
134: 
135: ### Server-Side Tracking Health Score
136: 
137: ```
138: Server-Side Tracking Health Score: XX/100 (Grade: X)
139: 
140: sGTM Pipeline:               XX/100  ████████░░  (20%)
141: CAPI / CAPI Gateway:         XX/100  ██████████  (25%)
142: Deduplication:               XX/100  █████████░  (15%)
143: Server-Side Hit Ratio:       XX/100  ████████░░  (15%)
144: Pixel Debug (6 events):      XX/100  ███████░░░  (10%)
145: Hash Quality / PII Handling: XX/100  ██████░░░░  (15%)
146: ```
147: 
148: ### Deliverables
149: 
150: - `SERVER-SIDE-TRACKING-AUDIT.md`: Full pipeline findings
151: - Test-event reproduction log (which events validated end-to-end on which
152:   date, with screenshots from Events Manager / DebugView)
153: - EMQ improvement roadmap (parameter-by-parameter)
154: - Hit-ratio dashboard recommendation
155: - Pre-launch checklist for any new platform integration (Amazon Marketing
156:   Cloud, Apple Ads, TikTok Events API)

(End of file - total 156 lines)
</content>

2: name: ads-creative
3: description: "Cross-platform creative quality audit covering ad copy, video, image, and format diversity across all platforms. Detects creative fatigue, evaluates platform-native compliance, scores creative diversity for Andromeda Entity-ID retrieval, and provides production priorities. Use when user says creative audit, ad creative, creative fatigue, creative diversity score, ad variation audit, ad copy, ad design, or creative review."
4: user-invokable: false

7: ---
8: 
9: <!-- Updated: 2026-04-13 | v1.5 -->
10: 
11: # Cross-Platform Creative Quality Audit
12: 
13: ## Process
14: 
15: 1. Collect creative assets or performance data from active platforms
16: 2. Read `ads/references/platform-specs.md` for creative specifications
17: 3. Read `ads/references/benchmarks.md` for CTR/engagement benchmarks
18: 4. Read `ads/references/scoring-system.md` for weighted scoring algorithm
19: 5. **Validate**: confirm at least one platform has creative data (assets or performance metrics) before proceeding
20: 6. Evaluate creative quality per platform
21: 7. Assess cross-platform creative consistency
22: 8. **Validate**: verify fatigue signals reference actual performance trends, not assumptions
23: 9. Generate production priority recommendations
24: 
25: ## Per-Platform Assessment
26: 
27: ### Google Ads Creative
28: - RSA: ≥8 unique headlines, ≥3 descriptions per ad group
29: - RSA ad strength: "Good" or "Excellent"
30: - Pin usage: minimal and strategic (over-pinning kills RSA flexibility)
31: - Extensions: sitelinks (≥4), callouts (≥4), structured snippets, image
32: - PMax asset groups: text + image + video + optional product feed
33: - YouTube: video quality, hook, subtitles (see ads-youtube sub-skill)
34: 
35: ### Meta Ads Creative
36: - Format diversity: ≥3 formats active (image, video, carousel, collection)
37: - Creative volume: ≥5 creatives per ad set
38: - Fatigue detection: CTR declining >20% over 14 days = FAIL
39: - Video length: 15s max Stories/Reels, 30s max Feed
40: - UGC/testimonial content tested
41: - Advantage+ Creative enhancements enabled
42: - Headline under 40 chars, primary text under 125 chars
43: 
44: ### LinkedIn Creative
45: - Thought Leader Ads active, ≥30% budget for B2B
46: - Format diversity: ≥2 formats tested (single image, carousel, video, document)
47: - Video ads tested
48: - Creative refresh: every 4-6 weeks
49: - Professional tone appropriate for platform
50: 
51: ### TikTok Creative
52: - ≥6 creatives per ad group (Critical requirement)
53: - All video 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 (non-negotiable)
54: - Native-looking content (not corporate)
55: - Hook in first 1-2 seconds
56: - No creative active >7 days with declining CTR
57: - Spark Ads tested (~3% CTR vs ~2% standard)
58: - Sound-on optimization (never silent)
59: - Safe zone compliance: X:40-940, Y:150-1470
60: - Trending audio used
61: 
62: ### Microsoft Creative
63: - RSA: ≥8 headlines, ≥3 descriptions
64: - Multimedia Ads tested (unique rich format)
65: - Ad copy optimized for Bing demographics (older, higher income, professional)
66: - Action Extension utilized (unique to Microsoft)
67: - Filter Link Extension tested
68: 
69: ## Creative Fatigue Detection
70: 
71: ### Signals of Fatigue
72: | Signal | Threshold | Action |
73: |--------|-----------|--------|
74: | CTR declining | >20% over 14 days | Refresh creative |
75: | Frequency (Meta) | >5.0 prospecting, >12.0 retargeting | New audience or creative |
76: | Watch time declining (TikTok) | <3s average | New hook needed |
77: | QS declining (Google) | Drop of 2+ points | Refresh ad copy |
78: | Engagement rate drop | >30% decline | Full creative overhaul |
79: 
80: ### Refresh Cadence by Platform
81: | Platform | Recommended Refresh |
82: |----------|-------------------|
83: | TikTok | Every 7-10 days (fastest fatigue) |
84: | Meta | Every 14-21 days |
85: | LinkedIn | Every 4-6 weeks |
86: | Google Search | Every 8-12 weeks |
87: | Microsoft | Every 8-12 weeks |
88: | YouTube | Every 4-8 weeks |
89: 
90: ## Format Diversity Matrix
91: 
92: Evaluate which formats are active per platform:
93: 
94: | Format | Google | Meta | LinkedIn | TikTok | Microsoft |
95: |--------|--------|------|----------|--------|-----------|
96: | Static Image | RSA image ext | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Multimedia |
97: | Video | YouTube, PMax | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ (required) | ✅ (9:16 vertical, April 2025) |
98: | Carousel | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
99: | Collection | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
100: | Document | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
101: | Shopping | PMax, Shopping | Catalog | ❌ | Shop | Shopping |
102: 
103: **Apple Ads Format Support:**
104: 
105: | Format | Apple Ads |
106: |--------|-----------|
107: | Static Image | ✅ (Custom Product Pages) |
108: | Video | ✅ (App preview videos) |
109: | Search Tab | ✅ (banner) |
110: | Today Tab | ✅ (editorial-style) |
111: 
112: ## Andromeda Creative Similarity Score (Meta)
113: 
114: Meta's Andromeda engine (launched October 2025) clusters ads with >60% similarity and suppresses delivery
115: of near-identical creatives ([Confect](https://confect.io/tactics/meta-andromeda-2026); [Meta Engineering](https://engineering.fb.com/2024/12/02/production-engineering/meta-andromeda-advantage-automation-next-gen-personalized-ads-retrieval-engine/)).
116: 100 minor variations (color swaps, small text changes) perform no better than 10 genuinely distinct creatives. Evaluate:
117: - Are creatives genuinely diverse in concept, angle, and format?
118: - Flag accounts relying on iterative variations rather than distinct concepts
119: - Recommend testing fundamentally different hooks, formats, and value propositions
120: 
121: ## Symphony Automation Awareness (TikTok, 2025)
122: 
123: TikTok's Symphony AI (2025) generates creative variations from product URLs. Assess:
124: - Whether account uses AI-generated vs original creatives
125: - Quality and performance comparison of Symphony vs manual creatives
126: - Impact on creative refresh strategy (Symphony can accelerate iteration)
127: 
128: ## Creative Health Scoring Weights
129: 
130: ```
131: Format Diversity:      25%  ████████░░
132: Fatigue Signals:       25%  ████████░░
133: Platform Compliance:   20%  ██████░░░░
134: Refresh Cadence:       15%  █████░░░░░
135: Volume:                15%  █████░░░░░
136: ```
137: 
138: Grade: A (90-100), B (75-89), C (60-74), D (40-59), F (<40).
139: 
140: ## Creative Check IDs
141: 
142: | ID | Check | Severity |
143: |----|-------|----------|
144: | CR-01 | Format diversity: >=3 formats per platform | High |
145: | CR-02 | Creative volume: meets platform minimums | High |
146: | CR-03 | Fatigue detection: CTR/engagement declining past thresholds | Critical |
147: | CR-04 | Refresh cadence: within platform-recommended cycle | High |
148: | CR-05 | Platform compliance: specs, safe zones, text limits | Critical |
149: | CR-06 | Hook quality: first 1-5s engagement (video) or headline impact (static) | High |
150: | CR-07 | UGC ratio: UGC/testimonial content tested on Meta and TikTok | Medium |
151: | CR-08 | Video specs: codec, resolution, aspect ratio per platform | Medium |
152: | CR-09 | Safe zone compliance: critical elements within 900x1000px usable area | Medium |
153: | CR-10 | Andromeda diversity: genuinely distinct concepts, not iterative variations (Meta) | High |
154: 
155: ## Universal Creative Best Practices
156: 
157: ### Cross-Platform Safe Zone
158: - 900x1000px usable area works across all vertical placements
159: - Keep critical elements centered and within safe margins
160: - Test on mobile devices (75%+ of ad impressions are mobile)
161: 
162: ### Ad Copy Principles
163: - Lead with benefit, not feature
164: - Include clear CTA (what should they do next?)
165: - Match ad message to landing page (message match)
166: - Use numbers and specifics over vague claims
167: - Test emotional vs rational appeals
168: 
169: ### Video Production Standards
170: - H.264 codec, AAC audio, MP4 container
171: - Minimum 720p (1080p preferred)
172: - Subtitles/captions always (accessibility + sound-off viewing)
173: - Brand mention within first 5s (awareness) or at CTA (performance)
174: 
175: ## Output
176: 
177: ### Creative Quality Report
178: 
179: ```
180: Cross-Platform Creative Health
181: 
182: Google:     ████████░░  X/X checks passing
183: Meta:       ██████████  X/X checks passing
184: LinkedIn:   ███████░░░  X/X checks passing
185: TikTok:     █████░░░░░  X/X checks passing
186: Microsoft:  ████████░░  X/X checks passing
187: ```
188: 
189: ### Deliverables
190: - `CREATIVE-AUDIT-REPORT.md`: Per-platform creative assessment
191: - Fatigue alerts (any creative past refresh cadence)
192: - Format diversity gaps per platform
193: - Production priority list (most impactful creative to produce next)
194: - Quick Wins (format conversions, CTA changes, Spark Ads setup)

(End of file - total 194 lines)
</content>

10: 
11: # Landing Page Quality for Ad Campaigns
12: 
13: ## Process
14: 
15: 1. Collect landing page URLs from active ad campaigns
16: 2. Read `ads/references/benchmarks.md` for conversion rate benchmarks
17: 3. Read `ads/references/conversion-tracking.md` for pixel/tag verification
18: 4. Assess each landing page for ad-specific quality factors
19: 5. Score landing pages and identify improvement opportunities
20: 6. Generate recommendations prioritized by conversion impact
21: 
22: ## Message Match Assessment
23: 
24: The #1 landing page issue in ad campaigns: does the page match the ad?
25: 
26: ### What to Check
27: - **Headline match**: landing page H1 reflects ad copy headline/keyword
28: - **Offer match**: promoted offer (price, discount, trial) is visible above fold
29: - **CTA match**: landing page CTA matches ad's promised action
30: - **Visual match**: consistent imagery between ad creative and page
31: - **Keyword match**: search keyword appears naturally in page content
32: 
33: ### Message Match Scoring
34: | Level | Description | Score |
35: |-------|-------------|-------|
36: | Exact match | Headline, offer, CTA all align perfectly | 100% |
37: | Partial match | Headline matches but offer/CTA differs | 60% |
38: | Weak match | Generic page, loosely related to ad | 30% |
39: | Mismatch | Page content doesn't reflect ad promise | 0% |
40: 
41: ## Page Speed Assessment
42: 
43: Slow pages kill conversion rates. For every 1s delay, CVR drops ~7%.
44: 
45: ### Thresholds (Ad Landing Pages)
46: | Metric | Pass | Warning | Fail |
47: |--------|------|---------|------|
48: | LCP | <2.5s | 2.5-4.0s | >4.0s |
49: | INP | <200ms | 200-500ms | >500ms |
50: | CLS | <0.1 | 0.1-0.25 | >0.25 |
51: | Time to Interactive | <3.0s | 3.0-5.0s | >5.0s |
52: | Page weight | <2MB | 2-5MB | >5MB |
53: 
54: ### Common Speed Issues in Ad Pages
55: - Hero images not compressed (use WebP/AVIF)
56: - Too many third-party scripts (chat widgets, analytics, heatmaps)
57: - Render-blocking CSS/JS above fold
58: - No lazy loading for below-fold content
59: - Font files not preloaded
60: 
61: ## Mobile Experience
62: 
63: 75%+ of ad clicks come from mobile. Mobile experience is critical.
64: 
65: ### Mobile Checklist
66: - Tap targets: ≥48x48px with ≥8px spacing
67: - Font size: ≥16px body text (no pinch-to-zoom needed)
68: - Form fields: properly sized, keyboard type matches input (email, phone, number)
69: - CTA button: full-width on mobile, visible without scrolling
70: - No horizontal scroll
71: - Images responsive and properly sized
72: - Phone number clickable (tel: link)
73: - No interstitials or popups blocking content on load
74: 
75: ## Trust Signals
76: 
77: ### Above-the-Fold Trust Elements
78: - Company logo visible
79: - Social proof (customer count, reviews, ratings)
80: - Security badges (SSL, payment security, guarantees)
81: - Recognizable client logos (B2B)
82: - Star ratings or testimonial snippet
83: 
84: ### Below-the-Fold Trust Elements
85: - Full testimonials with names, photos, companies
86: - Case study highlights with specific metrics
87: - Certifications, awards, accreditations
88: - Privacy policy link
89: - Physical address/phone number (local service businesses)
90: 
91: ## Form Optimization
92: 
93: ### Form Length Impact on CVR
94: | Fields | Expected CVR Impact | Use Case |
95: |--------|-------------------|----------|
96: | 1-3 fields | Highest CVR | Top-of-funnel, free offer |
97: | 4-5 fields | Moderate CVR | Mid-funnel, qualified leads |
98: | 6-8 fields | Lower CVR | Bottom-funnel, sales-ready |
99: | 9+ fields | Lowest CVR | Only for high-value offers |
100: 
101: ### Form Best Practices
102: - Pre-fill fields where possible (UTM data, known info)
103: - Use multi-step forms for 5+ fields (progressive disclosure)
104: - Show progress indicator on multi-step forms
105: - Inline validation (don't wait until submit to show errors)
106: - Error messages are clear and helpful
107: - Submit button text is specific ("Get My Free Quote" not "Submit")
108: - Thank you page has clear next steps
109: 
110: ## Landing Page Health Score Algorithm
111: 
112: ```
113: Landing Page Health Score = (Message Match x 0.25) + (Page Speed x 0.25) + (Mobile x 0.20) + (Trust x 0.15) + (Form x 0.15)
114: ```
115: 
116: Each component is scored 0-100, then weighted. Final score maps to grade: A (90-100), B (75-89), C (60-74), D (40-59), F (<40).
117: 
118: ## Consent Banner Impact
119: 
120: Flag if any of the following are true:
121: - Consent banner covers the primary CTA on load
122: - Consent banner delays form interaction by >1 second
123: - Consent banner pushes critical content (headline, offer, CTA) below the fold
124: - Banner cannot be dismissed on mobile without scrolling
125: 
126: > **Consent Mode V2**: Verify Consent Mode V2 implementation for EU/EEA traffic to ensure tracking data quality. Without Consent Mode V2, conversion modeling is degraded and remarketing audiences shrink significantly.
127: 
128: ## Quick Wins
129: 
130: | Priority | Fix | Expected Impact |
131: |----------|-----|-----------------|
132: | 1 | Move primary CTA above the fold on all devices | +15-25% CVR |
133: | 2 | Reduce form fields to essential only (name, email, one qualifier) | +10-20% CVR |
134: | 3 | Add trust badges near CTA (security, guarantee, reviews) | +5-15% CVR |
135: | 4 | Optimize hero image (WebP/AVIF, <200KB, proper dimensions) | -1-2s load time |
136: | 5 | Fix mobile tap targets (>=48x48px with >=8px spacing) | +5-10% mobile CVR |
137: 
138: ## Ad-Specific Landing Page Elements
139: 
140: ### UTM Parameter Handling
141: - UTM parameters captured and stored (for attribution)
142: - Click IDs preserved: gclid (Google), fbclid (Meta), ttclid (TikTok), msclkid (Microsoft)
143: - Parameters passed to form submissions or CRM
144: 
145: ### Dynamic Content
146: - Dynamic keyword insertion in headline (Google Ads feature)
147: - Location-specific content for geo-targeted campaigns
148: - Audience-specific messaging (different pages for different segments)
149: - A/B testing active on key elements (headline, CTA, hero image)
150: 
151: ### Conversion Tracking
152: - Thank you page/event fires correctly for all platforms
153: - Form submission triggers conversion event
154: - Phone call tracking configured (if applicable)
155: - Chat/live agent triggers tracked as micro-conversions
156: 
157: ## Landing Page Quality by Platform
158: 
159: | Platform | Key Requirement | Notes |
160: |----------|----------------|-------|
161: | Google | QS component: landing page experience | Directly affects ad rank and CPC |
162: | Meta | Page load speed critical | Slow pages = Meta penalizes delivery |
163: | LinkedIn | Professional, B2B appropriate | Match LinkedIn's professional context |
164: | TikTok | Mobile-first mandatory | 95%+ TikTok traffic is mobile |
165: | Microsoft | Desktop-optimized matters more | Higher desktop % than other platforms |
166: 
167: ## Output
168: 
169: ### Landing Page Assessment
170: 
171: ```
172: Landing Page Health
173: 
174: Message Match:    ████████░░  XX/100
175: Page Speed:       ██████████  XX/100
176: Mobile:           ███████░░░  XX/100
177: Trust Signals:    █████░░░░░  XX/100
178: Form Quality:     ████████░░  XX/100
179: ```
180: 
181: ### Deliverables
182: - `LANDING-PAGE-REPORT.md`: Per-page assessment with scores
183: - Message match analysis per ad-to-page combination
184: - Page speed improvement priorities
185: - Mobile experience fixes
186: - Form optimization recommendations
187: - Quick Wins sorted by conversion impact

(End of file - total 187 lines)
</content>

Evaluates spend distribution, bidding strategy appropriateness, scaling readiness, and identifies campaigns to kill or scale. Uses 70/20/10 rule, 3x Kill Rule, and 20% scaling rule. Use when user says budget allocation, bidding strategy, ad spend, ROAS target, media budget, or scaling."

7: ---
8: 
9: <!-- Updated: 2026-04-13 | v1.5 -->
10: 
11: # Budget Allocation & Bidding Strategy
12: 
13: ## Process
14: 
15: 1. Collect budget and performance data across all active platforms
16: 2. Read `ads/references/budget-allocation.md` for allocation framework
17: 3. Read `ads/references/bidding-strategies.md` for strategy decision trees
18: 4. Read `ads/references/benchmarks.md` for CPC/CPA benchmarks
19: 5. Read `ads/references/scoring-system.md` for health score algorithm
20: 6. **Validate**: confirm spend data covers ≥14 days before evaluating kill/scale decisions
21: 7. Evaluate budget allocation, bidding strategy, and scaling readiness
22: 8. **Validate**: verify kill list candidates have sufficient data (≥20 clicks or ≥$100 spend) before recommending pause
23: 9. Generate recommendations with kill list and scale list
24: 
25: ## Budget Allocation Framework
26: 
27: ### 70/20/10 Rule
28: - **70%** on proven channels (consistent ROAS/CPA targets met)
29: - **20%** on scaling channels (showing promise, need more data)
30: - **10%** on testing channels (new platforms, audiences, creatives)
31: 
32: ### Platform Selection Matrix
33: 
34: | Business Type | Primary | Secondary | Testing |
35: |---------------|---------|-----------|---------|
36: | SaaS B2B | Google Search, LinkedIn | Meta, YouTube | TikTok, Microsoft |
37: | E-commerce | Google Shopping, Meta | TikTok, YouTube | Microsoft, LinkedIn |
38: | Local Service | Google Search, Google LSA | Meta | Microsoft, YouTube |
39: | B2B Enterprise | LinkedIn, Google Search | Meta | Microsoft, TikTok |
40: | Info Products | Meta, YouTube | Google Search | TikTok |
41: | Mobile App | Meta, Google UAC | TikTok | Apple Ads |
42: | Real Estate | Google Search, Meta | YouTube | Microsoft |
43: | Healthcare | Google Search | Meta | Microsoft, YouTube |
44: | Finance | Google Search, Meta | LinkedIn | Microsoft |
45: | Agency (clients) | Varies by client | N/A | N/A |
46: 
47: ### Budget Sufficiency Rules
48: 
49: | Platform | Minimum Daily | Learning Phase Budget |
50: |----------|--------------|----------------------|
51: | Google Search | $20/day | Sufficient for 15+ conv/month |
52: | Google PMax | $50/day | Sufficient for algorithm optimization |
53: | Meta | $20/day per ad set | ≥5x target CPA per ad set |
54: | LinkedIn | $50/day Sponsored Content | 15+ conversions/month |
55: | TikTok | $50/day campaign, $20/day ad group | ≥50x target CPA per ad group |
56: | Microsoft | No strict minimum | Sufficient for stable delivery |
57: 
58: ## Bidding Strategy Evaluation
59: 
60: ### Google Ads Bidding Decision Tree
61: 
62: ```
63: Start
64: ├─ <30 conversions/month?
65: │  └─ Use Maximize Clicks (cap CPC at benchmark)
66: │     └─ When >30 conv/month → Maximize Conversions
67: ├─ 30-50 conversions/month?
68: │  └─ Use Maximize Conversions
69: │     └─ When stable CPA → Target CPA
70: ├─ >50 conversions/month?
71: │  └─ Use Target CPA
72: │     └─ When revenue tracking → Target ROAS
73: └─ Revenue tracking active + >50 conv/month?
74:    └─ Use Target ROAS
75: ```
76: 
77: ### Meta Ads Bidding
78: - **Lowest Cost (default)**: best for volume, may have CPA variance
79: - **Cost Cap**: sets CPA ceiling, may reduce volume
80: - **Bid Cap**: maximum bid per auction, most control
81: - **ROAS Goal**: target return on ad spend
82: - **CBO vs ABO**: CBO for proven campaigns, ABO for testing
83: 
84: ### LinkedIn Bidding
85: - **Cost Per Send (CPS)**: for Message Ads
86: - **Manual CPC**: recommended starting point for cost control
87: - **Cost Cap**: efficiency at scale with predictable CPA
88: - **Maximum Delivery**: highest volume, most expensive. Use only for scale with established data
89: - **Target Cost**: for predictable CPA
90: 
91: ### TikTok Bidding
92: - **Lowest Cost**: maximize conversions within budget (volume)
93: - **Cost Cap**: set maximum CPA (efficiency)
94: - **Bid Cap**: maximum bid per impression
95: - Budget ≥50x CPA per ad group for learning phase exit
96: 
97: ### Microsoft Bidding
98: - Mirror Google strategy but bid 20-35% lower
99: - Manual CPC for low-volume campaigns (Enhanced CPC deprecated on Google March 2025; verify Microsoft availability before recommending)
100: - Target CPA / Target ROAS for automated (requires 15+ conversions/30 days)
101: 
102: ## v1.5 Bidding Innovations
103: 
104: ### Google AI Max for Search
105: - 14% avg conversion lift for non-retail brands at similar CPA/ROAS, up to 27% for exact/phrase-match-heavy campaigns ([Google Ads blog, May 2025](https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-ai-max-for-search-campaigns/))
106: - Independent data is more conservative: median +13% revenue / +16% CPA across 250+ campaigns ([ALM Corp](https://almcorp.com/blog/google-ai-max-search-campaigns-performance-cpa-revenue-study/))
107: - Requires strong negative keyword lists (AI Max casts a wider net)
108: - DSA likely consolidated into AI Max by Q2 2026
109: - Best for: accounts with mature conversion tracking and keyword history
110: 
111: ### TikTok Smart+ Modular Control (2025)
112: - Lock targeting, creative, budget, or placement independently (mix of manual and AI)
113: - TikTok platform median ROAS 1.41-1.67 in 2025; Smart+ delivers ~53% lift over standard campaigns ([TikTok for Business](https://ads.tiktok.com/business/en-US/blog/smart-plus-ai-performance-solution))
114: - October 2025 update added module-by-module control after early-2025 control complaints
115: - Best for: advertisers wanting partial AI control without full black-box
116: 
117: ### Apple Ads Maximize Conversions
118: - GA: February 26, 2026. AI-powered auto-bidder for Apple Ads
119: - Target CPA based on weekly average (not per-conversion)
120: - Daily budget must be >=5x target CPA for stable performance
121: - Two-week learning phase required; avoid changes during learning
122: - Currently supports installs only (not post-install events)
123: 
124: ### Meta Advantage+ Bidding
125: - Automatic optimization across all placements within Advantage+ Sales campaigns
126: - Budget automatically shifts to highest-performing placements in real time
127: - Best combined with broad targeting and high creative volume
128: 
129: ## Scaling Assessment
130: 
131: ### Ready to Scale (Green Light)
132: - CPA consistently below target for 2+ weeks
133: - ≥50 conversions per week (learning phase exited)
134: - CTR stable or improving
135: - ROAS above target
136: - No creative fatigue signals
137: 
138: ### 20% Rule
139: Never increase budget by more than 20% at a time:
140: - Week 1: $100/day → $120/day
141: - Week 2: $120/day → $144/day
142: - Week 3: $144/day → $173/day
143: - Monitor 3-5 days after each increase for performance stability
144: 
145: ### Scaling Methods
146: 1. **Vertical**: increase budget on winning campaigns (20% rule)
147: 2. **Horizontal**: duplicate winning campaigns to new audiences
148: 3. **Platform expansion**: add budget on new platforms
149: 4. **Geographic expansion**: test new markets/regions
150: 5. **Format expansion**: test new ad formats on same platform
151: 
152: ## Kill List Assessment
153: 
154: ### 3x Kill Rule
155: - Any campaign/ad group with CPA >3x target → **flag for pause**
156: - Review spend in last 14 days with no conversions → **flag for pause**
157: - Creative with CTR >50% below platform benchmark → **flag for creative kill**
158: 
159: ### Kill Decision Framework
160: | Scenario | Data Required | Action |
161: |----------|---------------|--------|
162: | CPA >3x target | ≥7 days data, ≥20 clicks | Pause immediately |
163: | No conversions | ≥$100 spend or ≥50 clicks | Pause and diagnose |
164: | CTR <50% of benchmark | ≥1,000 impressions | Kill creative, test new |
165: | ROAS <50% of target | ≥14 days data | Reduce budget 50% or pause |
166: 
167: ## MER (Marketing Efficiency Ratio)
168: 
169: ```
170: MER = Total Revenue / Total Marketing Spend
171: ```
172: 
173: - Assess blended efficiency across all platforms
174: - Target MER varies by business: 3x-10x depending on margins
175: - Use MER to evaluate overall health, not just per-platform ROAS
176: - Incrementality testing recommended for MER accuracy
177: 
178: ## Output
179: 
180: ### Budget & Bidding Assessment
181: 
182: ```
183: Budget Allocation Health
184: 
185: Allocation Strategy:  ████████░░  XX/100
186: Bidding Strategies:   ██████████  XX/100
187: Scaling Readiness:    ███████░░░  XX/100
188: Budget Sufficiency:   █████░░░░░  XX/100
189: ```
190: 
191: ### Deliverables
192: - `BUDGET-STRATEGY-REPORT.md`: Full allocation and bidding analysis
193: - Current vs recommended budget split (pie chart data)
194: - Bidding strategy recommendations per platform/campaign
195: - Scale list: campaigns ready for more budget
196: - Kill list: campaigns/ad groups to pause immediately
197: - MER analysis and trend
198: - Quick Wins for immediate budget optimization

(End of file - total 198 lines)
</content>

1: ---
2: name: ads-plan
3: description: "Strategic paid advertising planning with industry-specific templates. Covers platform selection, campaign architecture, budget planning, creative strategy, and phased implementation roadmap. Use when user says ad plan, ad strategy, campaign planning, media plan, PPC strategy, or advertising plan."
4: user-invokable: false
5: tested_date: 2026-05-17
6: tested_with: claude-code v2.x
7: ---
8: 
9: # Strategic Paid Advertising Plan
10: 
11: Strategic planning is where the **10-Principle Thinking Framework** (see
12: `ads/references/thinking-framework.md`) earns its weight. THINK and
13: CONNECT (Lateral) dominate — first-principles unit economics combined
14: with cross-platform leverage are what make a plan strategic rather than
15: prescriptive. FEEL gates the messaging and audience choices; ACCEPT
16: gates the constraints (budget, timeline, team capacity).
17: 
18: ## Process
19: 
20: ### 1. Discovery
21: - Business type, products/services, target audience
22: - Current advertising status (active platforms, spend, performance)
23: - Goals: brand awareness, lead generation, e-commerce sales, app installs
24: - Budget range (monthly/quarterly)
25: - Timeline and urgency
26: - In-house team capacity vs agency needs
27: 
28: ### 2. Competitive Analysis
29: - Identify top 3-5 competitors
30: - Analyze their ad presence across platforms (Google Ads Transparency, Meta Ad Library)
31: - Estimate competitor spend levels and platform mix
32: - Identify messaging themes and creative approaches
33: - Note keyword/audience gaps (opportunities competitors are missing)
34: 
35: ### 3. Platform Selection
36: - Load industry template from `assets/` directory
37: - Match business type to recommended platform mix
38: - Read `ads/references/budget-allocation.md` for platform selection matrix
39: - Read `ads/references/conversion-tracking.md` for tracking setup requirements
40: - Assess platform fit based on:
41:   - Target audience demographics per platform
42:   - Product/service type suitability
43:   - Budget requirements per platform (minimums)
44:   - Sales cycle length and attribution needs
45:   - Creative capabilities and content availability
46: 
47: ### 4. Campaign Architecture
48: 
49: #### Naming Convention
50: ```
51: [Platform]_[Objective]_[Audience]_[Geo]_[Date]
52: ```
53: Example: `META_CONV_Prospecting_US_2026Q1`
54: 
55: #### Campaign Structure Template
56: ```
57: Account
58: ├── Brand Campaign (always-on, protect brand terms)
59: ├── Non-Brand Prospecting
60: │   ├── Campaign 1: [Top Funnel - Awareness]
61: │   │   ├── Ad Group/Set 1: [Audience A]
62: │   │   └── Ad Group/Set 2: [Audience B]
63: │   ├── Campaign 2: [Mid Funnel - Consideration]
64: │   │   ├── Ad Group/Set 1: [Interest-based]
65: │   │   └── Ad Group/Set 2: [Lookalike/Similar]
66: │   └── Campaign 3: [Bottom Funnel - Conversion]
67: │       ├── Ad Group/Set 1: [High-intent]
68: │       └── Ad Group/Set 2: [Custom audience]
69: ├── Retargeting
70: │   ├── Website Visitors (7-30 days)
71: │   ├── Engaged Users (video views, social engagement)
72: │   └── Cart Abandoners / Form Starters
73: └── Testing
74:     └── New audiences, formats, or messaging
75: ```
76: 
77: ### 5. Budget Planning
78: 
79: #### Monthly Budget Distribution
80: Read `ads/references/budget-allocation.md` for the 70/20/10 framework.
81: 
82: | Tier | Allocation | Purpose |
83: |------|-----------|---------|
84: | Proven (70%) | Primary platforms with proven ROI | Revenue engine |
85: | Scaling (20%) | Platforms showing promise | Growth engine |
86: | Testing (10%) | New platforms or strategies | Innovation |
87: 
88: #### Budget Pacing
89: - Month 1-2: heavy testing, expect higher CPA (learning)
90: - Month 3-4: optimize based on data, tighten targeting
91: - Month 5-6: scale winners, kill losers, expand
92: - Ongoing: 70/20/10 maintenance with quarterly reviews
93: 
94: ### 6. Creative Strategy
95: 
96: #### Content Pillars
97: - **Pain Point**: address specific problems your audience faces
98: - **Social Proof**: testimonials, case studies, reviews
99: - **Product Demo**: show the product/service in action
100: - **Offer**: promotions, free trials, lead magnets
101: - **Education**: teach something valuable related to your product
102: 
103: #### Creative Production Plan
104: | Priority | Asset Type | Platforms | Quantity |
105: |----------|-----------|-----------|----------|
106: | P1 | Product/service videos (15-30s) | Meta, TikTok, YouTube | 5-10 |
107: | P2 | Static images with copy | Google, Meta, LinkedIn | 10-15 |
108: | P3 | Carousel/collection | Meta, LinkedIn | 3-5 |
109: | P4 | UGC/testimonial video | TikTok, Meta | 3-5 |
110: | P5 | Long-form video (1-3 min) | YouTube | 2-3 |
111: 
112: ### 7. Tracking Setup Plan
113: 
114: Before launching any ads, ensure tracking is configured:
115: 
116: | Platform | Client-Side | Server-Side | Priority |
117: |----------|------------|-------------|----------|
118: | Google | gtag.js | Enhanced Conversions, GTM SS | P1 |
119: | Meta | Pixel | CAPI | P1 |
120: | LinkedIn | Insight Tag | CAPI (2025) | P2 |
121: | TikTok | Pixel | Events API + ttclid | P2 |
122: | Microsoft | UET Tag | Enhanced Conversions | P2 |
123: 
124: ### 8. Implementation Roadmap
125: 
126: #### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2)
127: - Install all tracking pixels and server-side tracking
128: - Set up conversion events and goals
129: - Create campaign structure and naming conventions
130: - Build initial audiences (custom, lookalike/predictive)
131: - Produce first batch of creative assets
132: 
133: #### Phase 2: Launch (Weeks 3-4)
134: - Launch campaigns on primary platform(s) first
135: - Set conservative budgets and bidding (Maximize Clicks / Lowest Cost)
136: - Monitor daily for the first 7 days
137: - Verify conversion tracking is firing correctly
138: 
139: #### Phase 3: Optimize (Weeks 5-8)
140: - Analyze initial data (minimum 2 weeks of data)
141: - Adjust bidding strategies based on conversion volume
142: - Kill underperforming ad groups/creatives (3x Kill Rule)
143: - Launch secondary platforms
144: - Begin A/B testing (creative, landing pages, audiences)
145: 
146: #### Phase 4: Scale (Weeks 9-12)
147: - Scale winning campaigns (20% rule)
148: - Expand to testing platforms (10% budget)
149: - Implement advanced strategies (ABM, Shopping feeds, Smart+)
150: - Monthly performance reviews
151: 
152: ## Industry Templates
153:

# Strategic Paid Advertising Plan

Strategic planning is where the **10-Principle Thinking Framework** (see
`ads/references/thinking-framework.md`) earns its weight. THINK and
CONNECT (Lateral) dominate — first-principles unit economics combined
with cross-platform leverage are what make a plan strategic rather than
prescriptive. FEEL gates the messaging and audience choices; ACCEPT
gates the constraints (budget, timeline, team capacity).

## Process

### 1. Discovery

2: name: ads-competitor
3: description: "Competitor ad intelligence analysis across Google, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, Microsoft, and Apple Ads. Analyzes competitor ad copy, creative strategy, keyword targeting, estimated spend via Meta Ad Library / Google Ads Transparency Center / TikTok Top Ads, and identifies competitive gaps and opportunities. Use when user says competitor ads, ad spy, competitive analysis, competitor PPC, ad intelligence, Meta Ad Library, Facebook Ad Library, Google Ads Transparency, competitor creative, or competitor research."
